title: lithocarpus densiflorus f. lanceolata
intro: Lithocarpus densiflorus f. lanceolata, also known as the California laurel or canyon live oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus densiflorus f. lanceolata are attributed to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds, providing a range of health benefits including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin conditions.
